description: Real-time status tracker for all 51 jurisdictions (50 states + DC)

filing_office.name: Alabama Secretary of State, UCC Division
filing_office.role: Central state-level filing and retrieval center for UCC financing statements. Maintains over 500,000 documents and processes 300-500 statements daily. County Probate Offices handle only UCCs fixed to real property (land).

authorization_model.summary: Alabama has NO formal 'authorized searcher' program. UCC search access is open to the general public through: (1) free basic online search portal (no account required), (2) advanced online portal via Alabama Interactive (per-search fees, optional subscriber account), and (3) paper UCC-11 by mail. Optional subscriber accounts provide billing convenience but are not required.

requirements_for_authorization.application_forms: None required. No application or approval process exists for searching UCC records.
requirements_for_authorization.entity_eligibility: Open to any individual, business, or entity. No restrictions.
requirements_for_authorization.technology_requirements: Web browser with internet access. No special software or technical setup required.

online_portal_free.description: Free public search by debtor name or file number. No login or account required. Index only -- no document images.

online_portal_advanced.description: Full-featured search with document images through Alabama Interactive. Per-search fees apply. Available to subscribers (ACH) and non-subscribers (credit card).

in_person.description: Personal delivery of filings and search requests at 770 Washington Avenue, Suite 580, Montgomery, AL 36104. Cash accepted only for in-person transactions.

mail.description: Submit UCC-11 Information Request form by mail to PO Box 5616, Montgomery, AL 36103. Payment by check, prepaid account, or credit card on file.

electronic_filing.description: Online UCC filing through Alabama Interactive portal. Also supports IACA XML standard for B2B electronic filing by approved remitters.

third_party.description: No formal authorized third-party searcher program. Any company can access the public portals to conduct searches on behalf of clients.

contact_information.agency_name: Alabama Secretary of State, UCC Division

items_needing_outreach.topic: Alabama Interactive subscriber fee discrepancy ($95 vs $120/year)
items_needing_outreach.detail: Conflicting sources on annual subscription fee. Need to confirm the current rate.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call (866) 353-3468 or email support@alabamainteractive.org

items_needing_outreach.topic: XML B2B filing implementation guide
items_needing_outreach.detail: Alabama has adopted IACA XML standard but the jurisdiction-specific implementation guide is not publicly posted.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call UCC Division at (334) 353-0203 or email business.services@sos.alabama.gov to request the guide

items_needing_outreach.topic: Bulk data access / data licensing availability
items_needing_outreach.detail: No publicly advertised bulk UCC data download or licensing program. Need direct inquiry.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call UCC Division at (334) 353-0203 or submit a public records request

items_needing_outreach.topic: Subscriber approval requirements for UCC service
items_needing_outreach.detail: Some Alabama Interactive subscriptions require agency approval. Need to confirm if UCC service requires approval or is auto-approved.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call (866) 353-3468 or email support@alabamainteractive.org

notable_differences_from_other_states: Open access model -- no formal authorized searcher program (unlike Delaware)
Free basic search available with no account required (unlike Georgia which requires at least a free Limited-Use account)
Two separate fee schedules: paper (through UCC Division directly) and online (through Alabama Interactive)
Online services provided through state-contracted third-party portal operator (Alabama Interactive), not directly by the SOS
UCC-3 terminations filed at no charge
Supports IACA XML standard for B2B electronic filing
$500 minimum deposit required for prepaid account setup

filing_office.name: Alaska Department of Natural Resources (DNR) - State Recorder's Office, UCC Central File
filing_office.role: The State Recorder's Office administers the statewide recording system and the Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) Central File. The UCC Central File System maintains the public record of filed documents related to security interests in personal property. NOTE: Alaska is unique — UCC filings are handled by DNR Recorder's Office, NOT the Secretary of State or Lt. Governor's Office.

authorization_model.summary: Alaska has NO formal 'authorized searcher' program. UCC records are public records available for public viewing and/or purchase. Online index searching is free and open to anyone without an account. Online filing requires a free registration account on the UCC Online Filing portal. There is no subscription model, no tiered access, and no approval process.

for_searching.application_forms: None required for online index searches
for_searching.fees: Free for online index searches. Formal information request (UCC-11 equivalent): $15 per debtor name. Information request with copies: $25 per debtor name. Individual copies: $2 each. Certification: $5 added to copy fee.
for_searching.entity_eligibility: Open to the public — any individual or entity

for_online_filing.fees: No registration fee. Standard filing fees apply per transaction.
for_online_filing.entity_eligibility: Open to the public — any individual or entity

payment_notes: Checks payable to Department of Natural Resources
Fixture Statements are recorded documents subject to the Recording Fee schedule
Online search of the database index is free — formal certified search requests and copies incur fees listed above
Social Security or Tax ID numbers are NOT required on UCC filings

online_search.description: Public online search of UCC Central Index. Does NOT include cross-reference of UCCs filed at the district level. To see all related records for a financing statement, search by document number and click the Associated Document link.

online_filing.description: Free registration required. Accepts attachments to online filings. Standard filing fees apply per transaction. System upgraded 12/21/2022; existing users required to reset passwords.

mail.address: UCC Central File, 550 West 7th Ave., Suite 108A, Anchorage, Alaska 99501-3564
mail.description: Submit national UCC forms by mail. Alaska requires use of national UCC forms. Submit only ONE form per filing (ignore multi-form instructions on national forms). Standard fees apply.

in_person.closures: Anchorage and Fairbanks offices closed 2nd Wednesday of each month, 9:00 AM - 10:30 AM for training
in_person.bulk_filing_note: For 100+ documents, call to schedule an appointment outside office hours

third_party.description: No formal authorized third-party searcher program exists. Third-party filing/search companies can access the free public online search portal or submit filings via the online filing system, mail, fax, or in-person on behalf of clients. No special credentialing is required.

system_details.forms_required: National UCC forms (most recent revision as of 07/01/2023)
system_details.data_retention: Inactive UCCs purged annually — UCCs more than 1 year past their date of lapse are removed from the database on an annual schedule
system_details.central_index_limitation: UCC Central Index does NOT include a cross-reference of UCCs filed at the district level. New Financing Statements (other than fixture statements) can ONLY be filed in UCC Central.

contact_information.agency_name: Alaska Department of Natural Resources (DNR) - State Recorder's Office
contact_information.myalaska_support_hours: M, T, W & F 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM; Thursday 8:00 AM - 4:00 PM

items_needing_outreach.topic: Confirm whether free online search returns full document images or index data only
items_needing_outreach.detail: The DNR website was blocking automated access during research, so it could not be confirmed whether online search results include viewable/printable UCC document images or just index metadata.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Phone call to (907) 269-8873 or test the search portal directly at https://dnr.alaska.gov/ssd/recoff/ucc/search/

items_needing_outreach.topic: Verify whether a myAlaska account provides enhanced search features
items_needing_outreach.detail: A myAlaska account integration is referenced for 'Recorder's Office & UCC Online Search' but it is unclear if this provides capabilities beyond the basic free search portal.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Phone call to (907) 269-8876 or email dnr.ro_contact@alaska.gov

items_needing_outreach.topic: Confirm current fee schedule amounts (regulation last updated 2018)
items_needing_outreach.detail: Fee schedule is codified in 11 AAC 05.250, last updated Register 227 (July 2018). Amounts may have changed since then via subsequent regulatory updates not yet reflected on law.cornell.edu.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Phone call to (907) 269-8873 or visit https://dnr.alaska.gov/ssd/recoff/ucc/fees directly

items_needing_outreach.topic: Bulk/batch search or data export availability for commercial searchers
items_needing_outreach.detail: No information found about bulk search, batch processing, data feeds, or API access for high-volume commercial UCC searchers.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Phone call to (907) 269-8873 or email dnr.ro_contact@alaska.gov

items_needing_outreach.topic: District-level UCC filings and how to cross-reference them
items_needing_outreach.detail: The UCC Central Index explicitly does NOT include cross-reference of UCCs filed at the district level. It is unclear whether district-level UCC filings exist that would not appear in the central index (e.g., fixture filings).
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Phone call to (907) 269-8873 to clarify what filing types appear only at district level

notable_differences_from_other_states: Alaska UCC filings are handled by the Department of Natural Resources (DNR) Recorder's Office — NOT the Secretary of State or Lt. Governor's Office
No formal 'authorized searcher' program exists — fully open public access with no account required for searching
Online index search is completely free with no registration
Online filing requires free registration but no approval process or fees beyond per-transaction filing fees
The statewide system has only 2 physical offices (Anchorage, Fairbanks) serving 34 recording districts
UCC Central Index does not cross-reference district-level filings — searchers must check both central and district records for complete coverage
Inactive UCCs are purged annually (1+ year past lapse date), unlike some states that retain indefinitely
Social Security and Tax ID numbers are explicitly NOT required on Alaska UCC filings

filing_office.name: Arizona Secretary of State

authorization_model.summary: Arizona does NOT have a formal Authorized Searcher program. Free, unrestricted online search portal available 24/7 to anyone. No registration required. For electronic filing, a prepaid account must be established.

online_portal.description: Free public UCC search - uncertified (watermarked) results

certified_search.method: UCC-11 Search Request form by mail or in-person

electronic_filing.description: PDF upload for filing (requires prepaid account)

in_person.phoenix: 1700 W Washington St, Fl 2, Phoenix, AZ 85007-2808
in_person.tucson: 400 West Congress Street, Suite 221, Tucson, AZ 85701

outreach_needed: Full list of 5 bulk data index options

filing_office.name: Arkansas Secretary of State

authorization_model.summary: No formal authorized searcher program. Online access requires an INA (Information Network of Arkansas) subscriber account ($150/year). Paper searches via UCC-11 are available to the public without subscription. Per Filing Office Rules, services provided on nondiscriminatory basis.

online_portal.description: Online UCC search - requires INA subscription

mail.address: Victory Building, 1401 W. Capitol Avenue, Suite 250, Little Rock, AR 72201

in_person.address: Victory Building, 1401 W. Capitol Avenue, Suite 250, Little Rock, AR 72201

outreach_needed: Exact fields required on INA subscriber registration form
Bulk data download pricing and format
Whether $6/search fee applies in addition to subscription
Turnaround time for paper UCC-11 requests

filing_office.name: California Secretary of State

authorization_model.summary: California does NOT have a formal authorized searcher program. UCC records are publicly accessible. Free informal searches available to anyone; certified searches require a fee. Filing requires a free bizfile Online account.

online_portal.description: bizfile Online - free UCC search by debtor name, secured party, or file number

outreach_needed: Prepaid customer account setup and minimum deposit
Bulk UCC data availability and pricing

filing_office.name: Colorado Secretary of State

authorization_model.summary: Fully open-access model. ALL UCC searches are FREE, including certified searches. No account needed for basic searches. Free optional account unlocks certified search reports. No authorized searcher program. Online-only system (no paper filings accepted).

online_portal.description: Colorado SOS UCC portal - free standard, advanced, and certified searches

bulk_data.description: Colorado Information Marketplace - free full UCC datasets with Socrata API

outreach_needed: Prepaid account minimum deposit details (low priority)
UCC master file request process and format (low priority)

filing_office.name: Connecticut Secretary of the State

authorization_model.summary: Open Public Access model. No formal authorized searcher program, no subscription required for searching. Free search available at service.ct.gov portal. Filing requires a free business.ct.gov account.

online_portal.description: Free lien records search by debtor name or lien number - no login required

bulk_data.description: Free CT Open Data Portal with nightly updates, JSON/CSV/API (Socrata)

outreach_needed: Confirm current filing fees (third-party sources vary)

filing_office.name: Division of Corporations, Delaware Department of State
filing_office.role: Central UCC filing office for the state. Handles all UCC filings and maintains the UCC index. All filings must be submitted electronically since December 1, 2015.

authorization_model.summary: Delaware has a formal, MANDATORY Authorized Searcher program. Since December 1, 2001, ALL non 'Search to Reflect' UCC debtor searches must be performed by a state-certified Authorized Searcher. There is NO public search portal. The program appears closed/invitation-only, limited to Registered Agents, Service Companies, and Law Firms with long-standing relationships with the state. Currently 25 certified searchers.

requirements_for_authorization.application_forms: NOT publicly documented. No application form or process published on the Division of Corporations website.
requirements_for_authorization.entity_eligibility: Restricted to Registered Agents, Service Companies, and Law Firms with a 'long-standing relationship with the State of Delaware'
requirements_for_authorization.technology_requirements: Unknown -- authorized searchers have 'special access to the state's internal lien index' but technical requirements not published
requirements_for_authorization.training: Unknown -- entities must be 'Certified by the Delaware Division of Corporations in performing online UCC filings and UCC searches'
requirements_for_authorization.confidence: Medium -- eligibility criteria partially documented but application process is opaque

online_portal.description: UCC searches: MUST go through Authorized Searcher (no public access). UCC filings: Can be submitted directly via e-UCC web application or XML submitters by anyone.

in_person.description: Not available for UCC searches. In-person visits to the Division of Corporations are by appointment only (call 302-739-3077).

mail.description: Not accepted for UCC filings since December 2015. UCC searches must go through Authorized Searchers.

third_party.description: MANDATORY for searches. All UCC debtor searches must be conducted through one of 25 state-certified Authorized Searchers. Each charges their own service fees on top of state fees.

contact_information.agency_name: Delaware Department of State, Division of Corporations
contact_information.physical_address: John G. Townsend Building, 401 Federal Street, Suite 4, Dover, DE 19901
contact_information.mailing_address: Division of Corporations, PO Box 898, Dover, DE 19903

items_needing_outreach.topic: Application process to become an Authorized Searcher
items_needing_outreach.detail: No public application form or documented process. Must contact the Division directly to inquire.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Email DOSDOC_ucc@delaware.gov or call (302) 739-3073 option 4

items_needing_outreach.topic: Whether new Authorized Searcher applications are being accepted
items_needing_outreach.detail: The 'long-standing relationship' language suggests this may be a closed program. Need to confirm if new entrants are accepted.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Email DOSDOC_ucc@delaware.gov or call (302) 739-3073 option 4

items_needing_outreach.topic: UCC Administrative Rules PDF content review
items_needing_outreach.detail: The PDF at corpfiles.delaware.gov/uccadminrules.pdf could not be parsed and may contain additional certification requirements.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Download and manually review, or request a copy from the Division

items_needing_outreach.topic: Technology/system access details for Authorized Searchers
items_needing_outreach.detail: Authorized Searchers have special access to the state's internal lien index but technical requirements are not documented.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Ask during outreach call/email

notable_differences_from_other_states: No public UCC search portal -- one of very few states requiring mandatory use of private authorized searchers
Only certified searches available -- no plain/uncertified search option
Strict exact-name search logic -- no fuzzy matching or name variations included
Authorized Searcher program appears closed/invitation-only with no published application process
All UCC filings must be electronic since December 2015 -- no paper filings accepted directly
Mandatory 24-hour expedited fee ($25) on all searches in addition to the $25 search fee
Paper UCC filings can only be submitted through Authorized Filers (separate from Authorized Searchers)

filing_office.name: DC Recorder of Deeds, Office of Tax and Revenue

authorization_model.summary: Authorization Model: Open Access with Registration (No formal authorized searcher program)

filing_office.name: Florida Department of State, Division of Corporations (Sunbiz) — operations privatized to FloridaUCC, LLC / Image API, LLC (now Docufree)
filing_office.role: The Florida Department of State is the statutory filing office for UCC filings under Florida Statutes Chapter 679. However, effective October 1, 2001, the administrative and operational functions were privatized under F.S. 679.527 to Image API, LLC (now Docufree). FloridaUCC, LLC, in partnership with the Florida Bankers Association, operates the Florida Secured Transaction Registry (FSTR). The Department of State retains sole and exclusive ownership of all materials and records, oversight/audit authority, and the right to reclaim the registry at any time. NOTE: Fixture filings and filings related to timber, minerals, and crops are filed with the Clerk of the Circuit Court in the relevant county, NOT with the state filing office.
filing_office.statutory_authority: Florida Statutes Chapter 679 (Uniform Commercial Code: Secured Transactions), specifically F.S. 679.527 (Florida Secured Transaction Registry)
filing_office.private_operator: Image API, LLC (now Docufree) / FloridaUCC, LLC

authorization_model.summary: Florida has NO formal 'authorized searcher' program. The Florida Secured Transaction Registry (FSTR) is statutorily required to be 'open to the public and accessible through the Internet' under F.S. 679.527. Searching the UCC database is free and does not require an account or login. Filing UCC documents online requires creating a free account on the FSTR portal. Frequent filers may establish ACH Debit Accounts for streamlined payment. As of April 17, 2005, FloridaUCC, LLC staff no longer perform searches on behalf of users — all searches are self-service via the website. A UCC XML API exists for programmatic/bulk electronic filing access, which requires a Web User Account.

for_searching.application_forms: None required — searching is free and open to the public without any account
for_searching.fees: FREE for online index/image searches. Certified information returns (UCC-11): approximately $15-20 per debtor name (exact fee needs verification).
for_searching.entity_eligibility: Open to the public — any individual or entity
for_searching.training: None required. Help documentation at https://floridaucc.com/help

for_online_filing.application_forms: Online self-service registration at https://www.floridaucc.com/OnlineFiling/Account/Register/ — email-based login, no approval process required
for_online_filing.fees: No registration fee. Standard filing fees apply per transaction.
for_online_filing.entity_eligibility: Open to the public — any individual or entity
for_online_filing.required_documents: None beyond registration form fields (email address required for account creation)

for_ach_debit_account.description: Frequent filers may establish an ACH Debit Account with FloridaUCC, LLC for streamlined payment processing
required_documents: Completed 'Authorization Agreement for Automatic Debits' form
Voided check from the designated bank account
for_ach_debit_account.approval_process: Submit form and voided check to FloridaUCC, LLC

for_xml_api_access.description: Programmatic filing access via UCC XML API. Requires Web User Account for authentication.
for_xml_api_access.documentation: UCC XML API Implementation Guide (PDF published by Florida Department of State)
for_xml_api_access.technical_details: HTTP POST to /FilingAsync endpoint. XML submission as string parameter. Returns DocumentReceiptID.
for_xml_api_access.enrollment_process: Not fully documented publicly — contact FloridaUCC, LLC for details

requirements_for_authorization.confidence: High for search/filing basics; Medium for ACH and API details

fee_schedule.statutory_authority: Florida Statutes section 679.525 (Processing fees) -- 2025 Florida Statutes
fee_schedule.note: Statutory fees confirmed from F.S. 679.525. The vendor (FloridaUCC, LLC) may charge different or additional operational fees. The FSTR fees page at floridaucc.com/fees requires JavaScript and could not be independently scraped. Call 850-222-8526 to verify current vendor fee schedule.

fees.ucc_1_initial_financing_statement_base: $25.00 (first page, includes cost of filing a termination statement) -- F.S. 679.525(1)(a)
fees.ucc_1_general_revenue_surcharge: $10.00 additional on first page of UCC-1 (deposited to state General Revenue Fund) -- F.S. 679.525(1)(h)
fees.additional_names_indexed: $3.00 per additional debtor, secured party, or assignee name -- F.S. 679.525(1)(c)
fees.termination_statement: Included in original UCC-1 filing fee (no additional charge) -- F.S. 679.525(1)(a)
fees.ucc_11_certified_search: Approximately $15-20 per request (exact vendor fee needs verification; older statute F.S. 15.091 set $20/name)

payment_notes: All UCC fees are nonrefundable processing fees
No refunds issued by FloridaUCC, LLC for documents that cannot be filed
Electronic filings processed immediately; paper filings 3-5 business days
ACH Debit Accounts available for frequent filers
Credit card payment available through online filing portal
fee_schedule.confidence: High for statutory fees from F.S. 679.525; Medium for vendor-specific operational fees

online_portal_search.database_coverage: UCC financing statement images and index data from January 1997 to present
online_portal_search.description: Primary search method. Free, public, no account required. IMPORTANT: Florida does NOT use standard search logic — the system displays the entire alphabetical index and lands on the page where the searched name would appear. All debtor name errors are fatal per the Florida Supreme Court's ruling in 1944 Beach Blvd., LLC v. Live Oak Banking Co.

online_filing.description: Online self-service filing portal created and maintained by Docufree. Features integrated payment processing. Requires free account creation (email-based login). Supports UCC-1, UCC-3, and related filings. Electronic filings are typically processed immediately.

xml_api_filing.description: UCC XML API for programmatic/bulk electronic filing. Filings submitted via HTTP POST to /FilingAsync endpoint. Returns DocumentReceiptID on successful submission. Requires Web User Account for authentication. HTTP statuses: 200 (queued), 400 (failed), 500 (server error). Filing statuses: InvalidXml, InProcess, InternalProcessingError, OK.
xml_api_filing.documentation: UCC XML API Implementation Guide (PDF published by Florida Department of State)
xml_api_filing.confidence: Medium — API guide found but full enrollment process not publicly documented

mail.address: Image API, LLC, UCC Filings, P.O. Box 5588, Tallahassee, FL 32314
mail.description: Paper UCC filings can be submitted by mail. Paper filings may take 3-5 business days for processing. UCC-11 Information Request forms (certified searches) can also be submitted by mail.

in_person.address: Image API, LLC, UCC Filings, 2002 Old St. Augustine Rd., Bldg. D, Tallahassee, FL 32301
in_person.hours: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM EST, weekdays, excluding state holidays
in_person.description: Filings and payments can be made in person at the Docufree-operated facility in Tallahassee.

third_party.description: No formal third-party searcher program. Third-party companies (e.g., Wolters Kluwer/CT Corporation, Florida Filing & Search Services, Inc.) routinely access the public FSTR portal to conduct UCC searches and filings on behalf of clients. No special credentialing required.

search_capabilities.database_coverage: UCC financing statement images and index data from January 1997 to present
search_capabilities.search_logic: NON-STANDARD — Florida does NOT use standard UCC search logic per IACA guidelines. The search displays the entire alphabetical index and positions results where the searched name would appear. This means ALL debtor name errors are fatal (per 1944 Beach Blvd., LLC v. Live Oak Banking Co.). An empirical study by Prof. Lynn M. Lopuk estimated that 32% of Florida UCC filings against registered organizations (over 230,000 filings) may be ineffective due to name errors alone.
search_capabilities.image_availability: Actual images of filed UCC documents viewable online since January 1997
search_capabilities.certified_search: UCC-11 Information Request — costs approximately $15-20 per filing. Each requires a separate UCC-11 form and fee. No batch processing available for certified information requests.
search_capabilities.staff_assisted_search: NOT AVAILABLE — as of April 17, 2005, FloridaUCC, LLC staff no longer perform searches. All searches are self-service.

statutory_authority.agency_name: Florida Department of State, Division of Corporations

private_operator.entity_name: FloridaUCC, LLC / Image API, LLC (now Docufree)
private_operator.mailing_address: Image API, LLC, UCC Filings, P.O. Box 5588, Tallahassee, FL 32314
private_operator.physical_address: 2002 Old St. Augustine Rd., Bldg. D, Tallahassee, FL 32301
private_operator.office_hours: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM EST, weekdays, excluding state holidays

items_needing_outreach.topic: Exact current fee schedule verification
items_needing_outreach.detail: The official fees page at https://floridaucc.com/fees and https://www.floridaucc.com/uccweb/fees.aspx both require JavaScript to render and could not be scraped. Secondary sources provide conflicting fee amounts ($20 vs $25 for UCC-1 electronic filing). The exact current fee schedule needs to be confirmed.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 or email help@FLORIDAUCC.com to request the current fee schedule. Alternatively, visit floridaucc.com/fees in a JavaScript-enabled browser.

items_needing_outreach.topic: UCC XML API enrollment process and eligibility
items_needing_outreach.detail: A UCC XML API Implementation Guide exists for programmatic filing, but the full enrollment/onboarding process, eligibility requirements, and any associated fees are not publicly documented. The guide mentions requiring a 'Web User Account Username' for authentication but does not detail how to obtain API access specifically.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 or email help@FLORIDAUCC.com to inquire about XML API access enrollment, requirements, and whether it supports bulk SEARCHING in addition to filing.

items_needing_outreach.topic: ACH Debit Account setup form
items_needing_outreach.detail: The ACH Debit Account option for frequent filers is referenced on the site but the actual 'Authorization Agreement for Automatic Debits' form could not be downloaded (JavaScript-rendered page). Need to obtain the form and confirm any minimum balance or volume requirements.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 or email help@FLORIDAUCC.com to request the ACH Debit Account authorization form and any related requirements.

items_needing_outreach.topic: Certified search (UCC-11) exact fee and turnaround time
items_needing_outreach.detail: The exact fee for a UCC-11 Information Request (certified search) and the current processing turnaround time could not be confirmed from public sources. Estimates range from $15-20 per request.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 to confirm the UCC-11 certified search fee, turnaround time, and submission process (online vs. mail only).

items_needing_outreach.topic: Bulk data download availability for UCC records
items_needing_outreach.detail: The Division of Corporations (Sunbiz) offers data downloads at https://dos.fl.gov/sunbiz/other-services/data-downloads/ for business entity data, but it is unclear whether bulk UCC filing data is included in the downloadable datasets.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Check the Sunbiz data downloads page and/or contact FloridaUCC, LLC to ask whether bulk UCC data exports are available for commercial use.

items_needing_outreach.topic: Fax number for FloridaUCC, LLC
items_needing_outreach.detail: No fax number was found during research. If paper submission via fax is desired, this needs to be confirmed.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call 850-222-8526 to ask if fax filings are accepted and obtain the fax number.

notable_differences_from_other_states: Florida's UCC filing system is PRIVATIZED — operated by FloridaUCC, LLC / Image API, LLC (Docufree) under contract with the Department of State since October 1, 2001, per F.S. 679.527
UCC searches are completely FREE and require NO account — unlike Georgia (which requires at minimum a free Limited-Use account) and unlike Delaware (which requires use of an Authorized Searcher)
No formal 'authorized searcher' program exists — the system is fully open to the public by statute
Florida does NOT use standard search logic — ALL debtor name errors are fatal per the Florida Supreme Court, unlike most other states that employ fuzzy/standard search logic per IACA guidelines

An empirical study found 32% of Florida UCC filings against registered organizations are potentially ineffective due to name errors — significantly higher error rate than states using standard search logic
Staff-assisted searches are NOT available (discontinued April 17, 2005) — all searches are self-service only
A UCC XML API is available for programmatic electronic filing (not just manual portal access)
Frequent filers can establish ACH Debit Accounts for streamlined payment — unlike many states that only accept per-transaction credit card payments
Florida is a TWO-PARTY consent state for call recording (F.S. 934.03) — relevant for any phone outreach to the filing office
The state retains sole ownership of all registry data and can reclaim the registry from the private operator at any time

realpc_action_items.explanation: No registration or authorization is needed to search Florida UCC records. Searches are free and open to the public. To FILE UCC documents, Real Finance would create a free account at https://www.floridaucc.com/OnlineFiling/Account/Register/. For high-volume filing, an ACH Debit Account can be established. For programmatic access, the UCC XML API is available.
recommended_next_steps: 1. Verify exact current fee schedule by calling 850-222-8526 or visiting floridaucc.com/fees
2. Create a filing account at https://www.floridaucc.com/OnlineFiling/Account/Register/ if UCC filing is needed
3. Inquire about XML API access if bulk/programmatic filing is desired
4. Consider establishing an ACH Debit Account if regular filing volume is expected
5. Test the search portal at https://floridaucc.com/search to verify access and familiarize with the non-standard search logic

filing_office.name: Georgia Superior Court Clerks' Cooperative Authority (GSCCCA)
filing_office.role: Statewide central index for UCC filings. NOTE: Georgia does NOT use the Secretary of State for UCC filings. Filings are submitted to the Clerk of Superior Court of any county, then indexed centrally by the GSCCCA.

authorization_model.summary: Georgia has NO formal 'authorized searcher' program (unlike Delaware). Access is open to any person or entity that creates an account on the GSCCCA portal. There are four account tiers with varying capabilities and fees. Anonymous access has been discontinued; a free Limited-Use account is the minimum required.

capabilities: Search Deed, Lien, Plat, and UCC indexes
View summary information for each instrument
account_tiers.upgrade_path: Can upgrade to Regular or Premium at any time

capabilities: Full search and image viewing access
limitations: Reactivation costs another $5.00 for a new 4-hour session

capabilities: Search Deed, Lien, Plat, and UCC indexes
View and print document images at $0.50/page
Can enable Certified Search capability (no extra monthly fee; $15.00 per debtor name per search)
limitations: No premium search features (e.g., address search, map search)
account_tiers.billing_notes: Monthly charge not prorated. Invoiced on 1st of each month. Past-due balances auto-charged to credit card on file.

capabilities: Certified Search capability available ($15.00 per debtor name)
Request and retrieve UCC Certified Search Reports ($15.00 per debtor name)
limitations: No access to Statewide Real Estate and UCC index browsing
Adding index access triggers $14.95/month Regular subscription fee
account_tiers.notes: Certified Search must be enabled by Master User in Account Management. Certified searches are issued ONLY by the Authority.

requirements_for_authorization.application_forms: Online self-service registration at https://account.gsccca.org/ — no paper application or approval process required
requirements_for_authorization.entity_eligibility: Open to any individual or entity. No restrictions on who can create an account.
requirements_for_authorization.required_documents: None identified. Account creation requires basic user information and credit card for paid tiers.
requirements_for_authorization.technology_requirements: Web browser with internet access. Google reCAPTCHA enabled for account registration security.
requirements_for_authorization.training: None required. Help documentation available at https://apps.gsccca.org/help/

online_portal.description: Primary access method. Subscription-based online portal with multiple search types: name search, file number search, daily search, secured party search, and certified search request.

in_person.description: Free access via search terminals installed in ALL 159 Clerk of Superior Court offices statewide. No account or fee required for in-person terminal use.

mail.description: UCC-11 Information Request forms can be submitted by mail. Certified searches may also be requested by mail.

efiling.description: Electronic filing portal for submitting UCC-1 and UCC-3 forms. Supports individual eFiling, bulk filing, and API integration for vendors.

third_party.description: No formal authorized third-party searcher program. However, third-party filing/search companies can and do access the GSCCCA portal with their own subscriber accounts to conduct searches on behalf of clients.

fans_notification_system.description: Filing Activity Notification System (FANS) allows users to monitor filings relating to them. Alerts when new UCC or other filings are indexed.

search_types: Certified Search (official, issued only by the Authority)
search_capabilities.database_coverage: UCC financing statement data from all 159 counties since January 1, 1995
search_capabilities.image_availability: Actual images of UCC statements can be displayed and printed (Regular/Premium accounts)
search_capabilities.turnaround_certified_search: 24 hours for online certified search requests

contact_information.agency_name: Georgia Superior Court Clerks' Cooperative Authority (GSCCCA)
contact_information.live_chat: Available via chat bubble on GSCCCA Contact page

items_needing_outreach.topic: UCC Administrative Rules (Rev. July 9, 2025) full text
items_needing_outreach.detail: The PDF at https://www.gsccca.org/docs/ucc-documents/georgiauccadminrules10july2013.pdf could not be parsed. The latest revision (July 9, 2025) should be obtained to confirm detailed filing rejection rules, search logic (exact vs. fuzzy matching), and any updated procedures.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call (404) 327-9058 or email help@gsccca.org to request a copy or confirm the URL for the July 2025 revision.

items_needing_outreach.topic: Bulk/API search access for high-volume commercial searchers
items_needing_outreach.detail: The eFiling portal mentions 'Vendor Integration requirements' for bulk filing and API access, but details on bulk SEARCH API access (vs. filing) are not publicly documented.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Contact GSCCCA to ask about API or bulk search access for commercial searchers conducting high-volume UCC searches.

items_needing_outreach.topic: Mail-based UCC-11 Information Request process
items_needing_outreach.detail: The UCC-11 form is referenced but the exact mail submission address, processing time, and fees for mail-based information requests were not confirmed on the website.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Call (404) 327-9058 to confirm mail-based UCC-11 submission address and processing timeline.

items_needing_outreach.topic: Escrow account setup for eFiling
items_needing_outreach.detail: Escrow accounts are listed as a payment option for eFiling but setup requirements and minimum balances are not documented publicly.
items_needing_outreach.recommended_action: Contact GSCCCA eFiling support to inquire about escrow account setup requirements.

notable_differences_from_other_states: Georgia UCC filings go through County Clerks of Superior Court (not the Secretary of State), with central indexing by GSCCCA
No 'authorized searcher' program exists — unlike Delaware, anyone can search by creating a free or paid account
Statewide single-county filing: filing in ANY one county provides statewide notification of lien position
159 county clerks feed into a single statewide GSCCCA index within 48 hours (24h clerk + 24h Authority)
Free in-person search terminals available at all 159 Clerk offices statewide
Certified searches are issued exclusively by the GSCCCA (not by individual county clerks)
